name: Minutes of the meeting of CWWG as on 13.10.2025

origin.filename: Minutes of the meeting of CWWG as on 13.10.2025.pdf

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Key Insights
texts.text: Key Insights

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 1. Kharif Sowing Status: As on 03 October, 2025, the area sown under Kharif crops is 1121.46 lakh ha as compared to 1114.95 lakh ha during the corresponding period of last year. About 102.3 percent of the normal area has been sown under the Kharif season crops. The status of sowing under Rice, Pulses, Coarse Cereals and Sugarcane is better than in the same period of last year.
texts.text: Kharif Sowing Status: As on 03 October, 2025, the area sown under Kharif crops is 1121.46 lakh ha as compared to 1114.95 lakh ha during the corresponding period of last year. About 102.3 percent of the normal area has been sown under the Kharif season crops. The status of sowing under Rice, Pulses, Coarse Cereals and Sugarcane is better than in the same period of last year.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 2. Weather Update :
texts.text: 2. Weather Update :

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: a) Temperature: Maximum temperatures is likely to be below normal in most of India except in East India, North East India and Chhattisgarh.
texts.text: Temperature: Maximum temperatures is likely to be below normal in most of India except in East India, North East India and Chhattisgarh.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: b) Rainfall Distribution: From October 01 -October 12 2025, the All-India cumulative rainfall is  40%  higher  than  its  Long  Period  Average  (LPA),  with  regional  variations:  (i)  East  & Northeast  India:  136%;  (ii)  Northwest  India:  192%;  (iii)  Central  India:  62%;  and  South Peninsula: (-) 50%.
texts.text: Rainfall Distribution: From October 01 -October 12 2025, the All-India cumulative rainfall is  40%  higher  than  its  Long  Period  Average  (LPA),  with  regional  variations:  (i)  East  & Northeast  India:  136%;  (ii)  Northwest  India:  192%;  (iii)  Central  India:  62%;  and  South Peninsula: (-) 50%.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · Heavy Rainfall very likely at isolated places over Coastal Andhra Pradesh, isolated places over Kerala & Mahe and Tamil Nadu Puducherry & Karaikal.
texts.text: Heavy Rainfall very likely at isolated places over Coastal Andhra Pradesh, isolated places over Kerala & Mahe and Tamil Nadu Puducherry & Karaikal.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · Isolated to scattered rainfall activity likely over South peninsular India.
texts.text: Isolated to scattered rainfall activity likely over South peninsular India.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · Thunderstorm with lightning over Odisha  on 13th &  14th;  Vidarbha  and  Chhattisgarh during 14th-16th October.
texts.text: Thunderstorm with lightning over Odisha  on 13th &  14th;  Vidarbha  and  Chhattisgarh during 14th-16th October.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · Overall, rainfall activity is likely to be above normal over south Peninsular India; below normal over northwest, east & northeast India and most parts of central India during the week
texts.text: Overall, rainfall activity is likely to be above normal over south Peninsular India; below normal over northwest, east & northeast India and most parts of central India during the week

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 3. Inputs Situation
texts.text: 3. Inputs Situation

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: a) Water Reservoir Status : As on 09 October 2025, the total live water storage in 161 important reservoirs across the country (monitored by CWC) is 166.737 billion cubic meter, which is 91.4% of total  live  storage  capacity  (182.479  BCM).  The  current  year's  storage  is  nearly 105.4 percent of last year's storage and 118.5 percent of the average storage of the last ten years  during  the  corresponding  period.  The  overall  storage  position  is  better  than  the corresponding period of last year in the country as a whole and is also better than the Normal storage during the corresponding period.
texts.text: Water Reservoir Status : As on 09 October 2025, the total live water storage in 161 important reservoirs across the country (monitored by CWC) is 166.737 billion cubic meter, which is 91.4% of total  live  storage  capacity  (182.479  BCM).  The  current  year's  storage  is  nearly 105.4 percent of last year's storage and 118.5 percent of the average storage of the last ten years  during  the  corresponding  period.  The  overall  storage  position  is  better  than  the corresponding period of last year in the country as a whole and is also better than the Normal storage during the corresponding period.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: b) Groundwater Levels : About 43.1% of the total monitoring wells show depth to water level in the range of 0 to 5 m below ground level and 37.6% show depth to water level in the range of 5 to 10 m below ground level. About 19.2% of the total monitoring wells show
texts.text: Groundwater Levels : About 43.1% of the total monitoring wells show depth to water level in the range of 0 to 5 m below ground level and 37.6% show depth to water level in the range of 5 to 10 m below ground level. About 19.2% of the total monitoring wells show

texts.label: text
texts.orig: water  level  deeper  than  10  m  below  ground  level.  The  groundwater  level  fluctuation analysis of Pre-monsoon 2025 with decadal mean of Pre-monsoon (2015-2024) indicates that 67.7% of analysed wells are showing rise and 33.0% are showing fall in water level. Remaining 0.3% stations analysed do not show any change in water level.
texts.text: water  level  deeper  than  10  m  below  ground  level.  The  groundwater  level  fluctuation analysis of Pre-monsoon 2025 with decadal mean of Pre-monsoon (2015-2024) indicates that 67.7% of analysed wells are showing rise and 33.0% are showing fall in water level. Remaining 0.3% stations analysed do not show any change in water level.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: As  per  the  Ground  Water  Drought  Indices  (GWDI),  in  pre-monsoon  2025,  90%  of  the analysed  wells  are  in  the  normal  category,  while  10%  of  the  analysed  wells  are  in  the drought category.
texts.text: As  per  the  Ground  Water  Drought  Indices  (GWDI),  in  pre-monsoon  2025,  90%  of  the analysed  wells  are  in  the  normal  category,  while  10%  of  the  analysed  wells  are  in  the drought category.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: c) Seeds  and  Fertilizers :  For  Kharif  2025,  certified  seed  availability  is  178.64  lakh  quintals against a requirement of 164.05 lakh quintals, ensuring a surplus of 14.60 lakh quintals. The fertilizer supply remains stable across all products.
texts.text: Seeds  and  Fertilizers :  For  Kharif  2025,  certified  seed  availability  is  178.64  lakh  quintals against a requirement of 164.05 lakh quintals, ensuring a surplus of 14.60 lakh quintals. The fertilizer supply remains stable across all products.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 4. Pests  &  Diseases :  There  is  no  major  pest  incidence  that  has  been  reported.  The  overall intensity of pests and diseases remained below the Economic Threshold Level. hu
texts.text: Pests  &  Diseases :  There  is  no  major  pest  incidence  that  has  been  reported.  The  overall intensity of pests and diseases remained below the Economic Threshold Level. hu

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 1. RAINFALL
texts.text: 1. RAINFALL

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · During the week (02 Oct -08 Oct 2025), higher rainfall was observed, as compared to Long Period Average (LPA) by 64%. Cumulative seasonal rainfall (01 st Oct to 08 Oct 2025) is higher than LPA by 60%.
texts.text: During the week (02 Oct -08 Oct 2025), higher rainfall was observed, as compared to Long Period Average (LPA) by 64%. Cumulative seasonal rainfall (01 st Oct to 08 Oct 2025) is higher than LPA by 60%.

texts.orig: Rainfall Forecast:
texts.text: Rainfall Forecast:

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · Overall, rainfall  activity  is  likely  to  be  above  normal  over  south Peninsular  India;  below normal over northwest, east & northeast India and most parts of central India during the week.
texts.text: Overall, rainfall  activity  is  likely  to  be  above  normal  over  south Peninsular  India;  below normal over northwest, east & northeast India and most parts of central India during the week.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Withdrawal of Southwest Monsoon 2025
texts.text: Withdrawal of Southwest Monsoon 2025

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · The line of withdrawal of southwest monsoon continues to pass through, Raxaul, Varanasi, Jabalpur, Akola, Ahilyanagar and Alibag.
texts.text: The line of withdrawal of southwest monsoon continues to pass through, Raxaul, Varanasi, Jabalpur, Akola, Ahilyanagar and Alibag.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · Conditions are favourable for further withdrawal of southwest monsoon from remaining parts  of  Maharashtra,  Madhya  Pradesh,  Uttar  Pradesh  and  Bihar;  entire  Jharkhand  and some  parts  of  West  Bengal,  Sikkim,  Chhattisgarh,  Odisha  &  Telangana  during  next  24 hours and from north east India during subsequent 1-2 days.
texts.text: Conditions are favourable for further withdrawal of southwest monsoon from remaining parts  of  Maharashtra,  Madhya  Pradesh,  Uttar  Pradesh  and  Bihar;  entire  Jharkhand  and some  parts  of  West  Bengal,  Sikkim,  Chhattisgarh,  Odisha  &  Telangana  during  next  24 hours and from north east India during subsequent 1-2 days.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 4. REMOTE SENSING BASED SOIL MOISTURE OBSERVATION
texts.text: 4. REMOTE SENSING BASED SOIL MOISTURE OBSERVATION

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Soil Moisture Observation
texts.text: Soil Moisture Observation

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · Soil moisture as on 03 Oct - 09 Oct 2025 was similar/better than the average of the past 9 years  in  major  parts  of  M.P,  Rajasthan,  Uttar  Pradesh,  Chhattisgarh,  Gujarat,  Jharkhand, West  Bengal,  Odisha,  Haryana,  Punjab,  Karnataka,  Kerala,  Maharashtra,  Telangana,  A.P. and Bihar.
texts.text: Soil moisture as on 03 Oct - 09 Oct 2025 was similar/better than the average of the past 9 years  in  major  parts  of  M.P,  Rajasthan,  Uttar  Pradesh,  Chhattisgarh,  Gujarat,  Jharkhand, West  Bengal,  Odisha,  Haryana,  Punjab,  Karnataka,  Kerala,  Maharashtra,  Telangana,  A.P. and Bihar.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · It was lower than average of past 9 years in pockets of Bihar , in pockets of eastern part of UP, in pockets of western parts of Assam, and in parts of Tamil Nadu.
texts.text: It was lower than average of past 9 years in pockets of Bihar , in pockets of eastern part of UP, in pockets of western parts of Assam, and in parts of Tamil Nadu.

texts.text: 5.   CROP SOWING STATUS FOR KHARIF SEASON

texts.orig: Kharif Crops - Area coverage
texts.text: Kharif Crops - Area coverage

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · Rice :  About  441.58  lakh  ha  (109.5%  of  Normal)  area  coverage  under  Kharif  Rice  as compared to 435.68 lakh ha during the corresponding period of last year.
texts.text: Rice :  About  441.58  lakh  ha  (109.5%  of  Normal)  area  coverage  under  Kharif  Rice  as compared to 435.68 lakh ha during the corresponding period of last year.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · Maize :  About  94.95  lakh  ha  (120.3%  of  Normal)  area  coverage  under  Maize  has  been reported as compared to 84.30 lakh ha during the corresponding period of last year.
texts.text: Maize :  About  94.95  lakh  ha  (120.3%  of  Normal)  area  coverage  under  Maize  has  been reported as compared to 84.30 lakh ha during the corresponding period of last year.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · Moong : About 34.87 lakh ha (97.6% of Normal) area coverage under Moong as compared to 34.96 lakh ha during the corresponding period of last year.
texts.text: Moong : About 34.87 lakh ha (97.6% of Normal) area coverage under Moong as compared to 34.96 lakh ha during the corresponding period of last year.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · Soybean : About 120.45 lakh ha (94.7% of Normal) area coverage under Soyabean has been reported  as  compared  to  129.55  lakh  ha  during  the  corresponding  period  of  last  year. Soyabean area diverted in Maize in MP and Maharashtra.
texts.text: Soybean : About 120.45 lakh ha (94.7% of Normal) area coverage under Soyabean has been reported  as  compared  to  129.55  lakh  ha  during  the  corresponding  period  of  last  year. Soyabean area diverted in Maize in MP and Maharashtra.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · Sugarcane :  About  59.07  lakh  ha  area  coverage  under  Sugarcane  has  been  reported  as compared to 57.22 lakh ha during the corresponding period of last year.
texts.text: Sugarcane :  About  59.07  lakh  ha  area  coverage  under  Sugarcane  has  been  reported  as compared to 57.22 lakh ha during the corresponding period of last year.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · Cotton : About 110.03 lakh ha (85% of Normal) area coverage has been reported compared to  112.97  lakh  ha  during  the  corresponding  period  of  last  year.  Cotton  area  diverted  in Paddy and Groundnut respectively in Punjab & Haryana and Gujarat.
texts.text: Cotton : About 110.03 lakh ha (85% of Normal) area coverage has been reported compared to  112.97  lakh  ha  during  the  corresponding  period  of  last  year.  Cotton  area  diverted  in Paddy and Groundnut respectively in Punjab & Haryana and Gujarat.
